title: oxytropis setosa subsp. setosa
intro: Oxytropis setosa subsp. setosa, also known as the hairy globeflower or hairy globe pea, is a flowering plant in the legume family, native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. Traditionally used in folk medicine, it is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and urinary tract infections. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, lowering blood pressure, and supporting the immune system, making it a sought-after natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
